title: Implement #1383 completion gate

execplan_profile.projection_rule: canonical_core is authoritative for intent, scope, next action, proof, continuation, and closeout; legacy fields remain compatibility projections.

canonical_core.requested_outcome: Implement #1383 by pivoting PR #1382 from packet-first intent visibility to a strict completion_gate closeout transition rule.
canonical_core.hard_constraints: Do not add a universal intent ledger or semantic classifier; reuse existing Planning, proof, acceptance, closeout, and rendering fields; AW enforces the transition rule while the agent owns semantic satisfaction judgment.
canonical_core.agent_may_decide: Compact gate field names, exact derived inputs, and focused regression shape that proves unsupported full completion claims are blocked.
canonical_core.escalate_when: A correct implementation cannot block completion claims without broad new state, PR-specific machinery, or AW-owned semantic classification.
canonical_core.next_action: Update PR #1382 body for #1383 after validation.
completion_criteria: Closeout trust and closeout_report expose completion_gate.
Full done/implemented/close-keyword claims are blocked when active human intent is unsatisfied and no explicit human partial exists.
Continuation, clarification, human-accepted partial, and satisfied intent branches are covered by focused regression tests.
Final response rendering obeys the gate and includes the gate when full completion is blocked.

goal: Create a bounded plan for Implement #1380 active intent satisfaction guard.
non_goals: Leave adjacent backlog or follow-on work out of this plan.

intent.outcome: Implement the #1383 completion gate pivot for PR #1382.
intent.constraints: Reuse existing closeout and Planning fields; do not make AW the semantic judge.
intent.latitude: Derived gate shape, compact rendering, schema/reference docs, and focused tests.
intent.escalation: Escalate if enforcement requires a new universal intent ledger or PR-specific closure engine.

execution.next_step: No required continuation remains for this archived slice.

invariants: Completion claims must follow completion_gate claim_level_allowed.

iterative_follow_through.what this slice enabled: closeout completion_gate enforcement across closeout_trust, closeout_report, and final-response rendering
iterative_follow_through.discovered implications: proof --current can still fall back to manual verification even after implement --changed selected and passed concrete proof commands
iterative_follow_through.next likely slice: none; pivot is ready for PR review

intent_interpretation.literal request: Pivot PR #1382 to a completion_gate that enforces intent satisfaction before closeout claims.
intent_interpretation.inferred intended outcome: Implement #1383 as the controlling design superseding #1380.
intent_interpretation.chosen concrete what: Derived closeout completion_gate plus final-response and compact closeout_trust enforcement.
intent_interpretation.review guidance: Confirm the scaffolded plan still matches the promoted item before broad implementation.

execution_bounds.allowed paths: workspace runtime/reporting support, workspace_report schema/reference docs, and report CLI tests.
execution_bounds.required validation commands: focused completion_gate tests; make test-workspace; make lint-workspace; contract tooling; structured inventory; maintainer surfaces; schema-reference docs.
execution_bounds.ask-before-refactor threshold: Ask before broadening beyond the promoted item.
execution_bounds.stop before touching: Unrelated backlog, adjacent modules, or canonical contracts not named by this plan.

stop_conditions.stop when: The work no longer matches the promoted item or its completion criteria.
stop_conditions.escalate when boundary reached: A correct fix requires changing the requested outcome or ownership boundary.
stop_conditions.escalate on scope drift: Implementation needs files outside the filled execution bounds.
stop_conditions.escalate on proof failure: The selected proof cannot demonstrate the completion criteria.

context_budget.live working set: This execplan, the promoted item, and the narrow files needed for the current implementation step.
context_budget.recoverable later: Repo background, historical reviews, and deferred backlog unless compact outputs point there.
context_budget.externalize before shift: Update execution_run, proof_report, finished_run_review, and closeout_distillation before pausing.
context_budget.pre-work config pull: Use compact config/startup/summary outputs before opening raw planning or routing files.
context_budget.pre-work memory pull: Route to the narrowest relevant memory only when the task needs durable repo knowledge.
context_budget.tiny resumability note: completion_gate is the new center; active_intent_contract/matrix are compatibility inputs, not the closeout authority.
context_budget.context-shift triggers: Proof failure, scope drift, interruption, handoff, or closeout.

delegated_judgment.hard constraints: Do not make AW the semantic judge and do not rely on caveated completion when active intent is unsatisfied.
delegated_judgment.agent may decide locally: Exact gate derivation from existing closeout fields, rendering wording, and focused test shape.
delegated_judgment.escalate when: The gate cannot be made strict without broad new state or PR-specific issue closure machinery.

post_decomposition_delegation.decision rule: After this slice is bounded, decide whether direct work, read-only exploration, implementation handoff, validation handoff, or stronger review improves quality or saves tokens safely.
post_decomposition_delegation.required evidence: slice id, route, reason, quality risk, token-saving class, read-first refs, write scope, proof burden, stop conditions, and return contract
post_decomposition_delegation.route skipped reason: PR #1382 review fixes are tightly coupled across workspace report, Planning closeout, schemas, and focused regressions; keep-local preserves context and does not reduce required proof.

system_intent_alignment.relevant system intent: Preserve the larger intended outcome separately from this bounded slice.
system_intent_alignment.slice shaping bias: Keep the slice bounded while carrying any larger follow-on through explicit continuation fields.
system_intent_alignment.broader-lane validation question: Did this slice advance the declared larger outcome, or only complete the local task?

active_milestone.scope: Pivot PR #1382 to the #1383 completion gate design.

immediate_next_action: Update PR #1382 body and push the completion_gate pivot.
invariants: If active_intent_satisfied is false and human_accepted_partial is false, full completion claims must remain blocked.
validation_commands: focused completion_gate pytest; make test-workspace; make lint-workspace; contract checks; maintainer surfaces; schema-reference docs.
completion_criteria: The #1383 completion gate pivot is implemented, validated, and ready for PR review.
